amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Chemotaxis in E. coli is a sophisticated sensory system that allows bacteria to navigate chemical gradients in their environment, moving toward attractants (positive chemotaxis) and away from repellents (negative chemotaxis). The system involves methyl-accepting chemotaxis proteins (MCPs) that detect chemical signals, the CheA-CheW signaling complex, and a two-component phosphorelay that modulates flagellar motor rotation. Adaptation through methylation and demethylation by CheR and CheB allows cells to respond to temporal changes in concentration. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from ligand binding through signal transduction, motor control, and the adaptation mechanisms that enable sustained directional movement.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Chemical Gradient] -->|Attractant| B[Serine, Aspartate]
    A -->|Repellent| C[Ni2+, Co2+]
    
    B --> D[MCP Receptors]
    C --> D
    D -->|Tsr, Tar, Trg, Tap| E{Ligand Binding?}
    
    E -->|Attractant Bound| F[MCP Conformational Change]
    E -->|Repellent Bound| G[Opposite Conformation]
    E -->|No Ligand| H[Basal State]
    
    F --> I[Reduced CheA Activity]
    G --> J[Increased CheA Activity]
    H --> K[Moderate CheA Activity]
    
    K --> L[CheA Histidine Kinase]
    J --> L
    I --> L
    
    L -->|Requires| M[CheW Coupling Protein]
    M --> N[Receptor-CheW-CheA Complex]
    N --> O{CheA Autophosphorylation?}
    
    O -->|High Activity| P[CheA~P]
    O -->|Low Activity| Q[Unphosphorylated CheA]
    
    P --> R{Phosphotransfer Branch?}
    R -->|To CheY| S[CheY~P]
    R -->|To CheB| T[CheB~P]
    
    S --> U{CheY~P Concentration?}
    U -->|High| V[Enhanced Motor Binding]
    U -->|Low| W[Reduced Motor Binding]
    
    V --> X[FliM FliN FliG Motor Complex]
    W --> X
    X --> Y{Motor Rotation?}
    
    Y -->|CheY~P Bound| Z[Clockwise CW Rotation]
    Y -->|CheY~P Unbound| AA[Counterclockwise CCW Rotation]
    
    Z --> AB(\1)
    AA --> AC[Smooth Swimming Run]
    
    AB --> AD{New Direction?}
    AD -->|Random| AE[Reorientation]
    AE --> AF{Gradient Assessment?}
    
    AF -->|Better| AC
    AF -->|Worse| AB
    
    AC --> AG{Continue Run?}
    AG -->|Favorable Gradient| AC
    AG -->|Unfavorable| AB
    
    S --> AH[CheZ Phosphatase]
    AH --> AI[CheY Dephosphorylation]
    AI --> AJ[/Rapid Signal Termination/]
    
    T --> AK[Active CheB Methylesterase]
    AK --> AL{Adaptation Mechanism?}
    
    AL -->|Demethylation| AM[Remove Methyl Groups]
    AM --> AN[MCP Glutamate Residues]
    AN --> AO[Reduced Sensitivity]
    
    D --> AP[CheR Methyltransferase]
    AP --> AQ{Constitutive Methylation?}
    AQ -->|Yes| AR[Add Methyl Groups]
    AR --> AS[MCP Glutamine Residues]
    AS --> AT[Increased Sensitivity]
    
    AO --> AU{Adaptation Balance?}
    AT --> AU
    AU -->|Equilibrium| AV[Restored Basal Activity]
    AV --> L
    
    F --> AW{Temporal Sensing?}
    AW -->|Increasing Attractant| AX[Sustained Low CheA]
    AW -->|Decreasing Attractant| AY[Sustained High CheA]
    
    AX --> AZ[Prolonged Runs]
    AY --> BA[Frequent Tumbles]
    
    AZ --> BB{Gradient Climbing?}
    BA --> BB
    BB -->|Successful| BC[Accumulation at Peak]
    BB -->|Unsuccessful| BD[Continue Searching]
    
    BC --> BE[Optimal Environment]
    BE --> BF[Enhanced Growth]
    
    D --> BG{Receptor Clustering?}
    BG -->|Cooperative| BH[Receptor Arrays]
    BH --> BI[Signal Amplification]
    BI --> BJ[Enhanced Sensitivity]
    BJ --> N
    
    N --> BK{Allosteric Coupling?}
    BK -->|Team Response| BL[Coordinated Activity]
    BL --> O

name: E. coli Chemotaxis
notes: Created based on classic and contemporary research on E. coli chemotaxis, including the foundational work by Berg and Brown and subsequent molecular studies.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on extensive peer-reviewed literature on E. coli chemotaxis, including studies on MCP structure and function, CheA kinase signaling, flagellar motor switching, and adaptation mechanisms. The pathway components, regulatory interactions, and logic gates have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents the canonical chemotaxis pathway that has served as a paradigm for bacterial signal transduction.

sources.title: Chemotaxis in Escherichia coli analyzed by three-dimensional tracking

sources.journal: Annual review of cell and developmental biology
sources.title: The two-component signaling pathway of bacterial chemotaxis: a molecular view of signal transduction by receptors, kinases, and adaptation enzymes

sources.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
sources.title: Receptor sensitivity in bacterial chemotaxis
